item.title: Temporary Refactoring and Improving Your Code Reading Skills
item.description: I was talking to a friend of mine, Scott, over Slack about reading code! Specifically, how to improve your code reading skills. I asked him what he usually does when he reads source code. What he said is interesting: He does a temporary refactoring. That’s it. He usually takes a long method that can be separated out and then he turns it into a separate method. For example- readEmployeeDataFromDatabase() This method can be broken into multiple methods, and he does that.
